items.text: So excited the Campus Community Garden reopens for spring! Open Tuesdays and Thursdays, 3:00–6:00 PM, behind Lowell Hall. A plot costs just $8 for the whole season — best deal on campus. Sign up with Ms. Reyes at the front desk. Come get your hands dirty!

questions.stem: According to the post, how much does a garden plot cost?
questions.explanation: The post says a plot costs just $8 for the whole season, which the correct option restates as eight dollars for spring.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about someone who wants to join the garden?

options.A: They would need to pay a monthly fee
options.B: They would need to garden every weekday
options.C: They would need to contact Ms. Reyes
options.D: They would need to bring their own tools

questions.explanation: Because the post says to sign up with Ms. Reyes at the front desk, a person wishing to join would have to reach out to her.

items.text: NOTICE: Unclaimed items in the Student Center Lost and Found will be donated to charity after Friday, June 6. Owners must retrieve belongings at the information desk, Room 120, between 8:00 AM and 5:00 PM on weekdays. A photo ID is required for release.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what will happen to items left after June 6?

options.A: They will be sold at auction
options.C: They will be discarded as trash
options.D: They will be given to charity

questions.explanation: The notice states that unclaimed items will be donated to charity after June 6, which the correct option paraphrases as given to charity.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must owners present to reclaim an item?
questions.explanation: The notice specifies that a photo ID is required for release, so owners must present a valid photo ID.

items.text: NOTICE: Heating and cooling systems in Carver Hall will undergo maintenance from Monday, March 9, through Wednesday, March 11. Indoor temperatures may fluctuate during this period. Occupants are advised to dress warmly. Work will occur between 7:00 AM and 4:00 PM daily. We appreciate your patience.

questions.stem: According to the notice, when will the maintenance take place?
questions.explanation: The notice states the work runs from Monday, March 9, through Wednesday, March 11, matching March 9 to March 11.

questions.stem: In the notice, the word "fluctuate" is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: Fluctuate means to shift up and down irregularly, so it is closest in meaning to vary; drop and rise name only one direction, and stabilize is the opposite.

items.text: NOTICE: The water fountains on the third floor of Hensley Hall will be shut off for pipe repairs from Thursday, October 2, at 6:00 PM until Saturday, October 4, at noon. During the closure, bottled water is available for $1 from a vending machine. Fountains on other floors remain operational.

questions.stem: According to the notice, when will the third-floor fountains be unavailable?

options.A: From Thursday morning until Saturday evening
options.B: From Friday evening until Sunday morning
options.C: From Thursday noon until Saturday night
options.D: From Thursday evening until Saturday midday

questions.explanation: The shutoff runs from Thursday at 6:00 PM (Thursday evening) until Saturday at noon (Saturday midday), so option D captures both endpoints correctly.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a person on the third floor who needs a drink on Friday?

options.A: That person could use the third-floor fountains normally
options.B: That person could buy bottled water for one dollar
options.C: That person could find free water on that floor
options.D: That person could wait for repairs to finish Friday

questions.explanation: Friday falls entirely within the closure, so the third-floor fountains are off; the notice says bottled water is available for $1, meaning the person could buy bottled water for one dollar.
